Root Canal Treatment in Kolkata | Painless, Save Your Tooth

Home/Services/Root Canal Treatment
Root Canal Treatment

Root Canal Treatment in Kolkata | Painless, Save Your Tooth

Root canal treatment has an undeserved reputation for being painful. At Sushmita's Dental Clinic in Salt Lake City (Bidhannagar), Kolkata, we use modern rotary endodontic techniques and effective local anaesthesia to make the procedure virtually painless.

A root canal is needed when the inner pulp of a tooth becomes infected. Without treatment, the infection spreads and the tooth must be extracted. Root canal treatment saves the tooth, eliminates pain, and prevents spread of infection.

Our experienced endodontist treats patients from all across Kolkata including Jadavpur, New Town, Howrah, Dum Dum, Behala, and Barasat at our Salt Lake Sector II clinic.

Key Benefits

  • Save your natural tooth from extraction
  • Eliminates toothache and infection
  • Modern rotary technique is quick and painless
  • Protects surrounding teeth and jawbone
  • Covered by many dental insurance plans
  • Long-lasting results with proper care

Our Treatment Process

1

Diagnosis

X-ray to assess the extent of infection and root anatomy.

2

Anaesthesia

Local anaesthesia applied for a completely comfortable experience.

3

Pulp Removal

Infected pulp tissue removed using precision rotary instruments.

4

Canal Cleaning & Shaping

Root canals are cleaned, shaped, and disinfected thoroughly.

5

Filling & Crown

Canals sealed and a crown placed to restore full tooth function.

Book Appointment

We confirm within 2 hours • Free first consultation

FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Is root canal treatment painful?

With modern techniques and effective local anaesthesia, RCT is virtually painless.

How many appointments does RCT require?

Most root canal treatments are completed in 1-2 appointments.

What happens if I don't get a root canal?

The infection will spread, causing severe pain, abscess, and eventual tooth loss.

How long does a root canal-treated tooth last?

With proper care and a crown, a root canal-treated tooth can last a lifetime.
